Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.","type":"number"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"add_labels","description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"noop","description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Updates the emitter package JSON with the new version\\n3. Regenerates SDK clients\\n4. Creates a PR with title \\u0026#34;Update {emitterName} version to prerelease {emitterVersion}\\u0026#34;\\n\\nThe management generator (http-client-csharp-mgmt) should have the same automated version bump behavior for `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion`.\\n\\n## Current State\\n\\n- `UnbrandedGeneratorVersion` and `AzureGeneratorVersion` are automatically bumped by CI pipelines\\n- `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` was recently added and is currently updated manually\\n- The mgmt generator CI (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml`) already uses the same `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` template, but the version bump for the NuGet package reference in `Directory.Generation.Packages.props` may not be wired up\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\nWhen a new version of the mgmt generator is published from the `http-client-csharp-mgmt` CI pipeline, `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` in `Directory.Generation.Packages.props` should be automatically updated, mirroring how the Azure Generator pipeline updates `AzureGeneratorVersion`.\\n\\n## Reference\\n\\n- PR comment: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56627#discussion_r2915155700\\n- Azure Updates the emitter package JSON with the new version\\n3. Regenerates SDK clients\\n4. Creates a PR with title \\\"Update {emitterName} version to prerelease {emitterVersion}\\\"\\n\\nThe management generator (http-client-csharp-mgmt) should have the same automated version bump behavior for `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion`.\\n\\n## Current State\\n\\n- `UnbrandedGeneratorVersion` and `AzureGeneratorVersion` are automatically bumped by CI pipelines\\n- `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` was recently added and is currently updated manually\\n- The mgmt generator CI (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml`) already uses the same `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` template, but the version bump for the NuGet package reference in `Directory.Generation.Packages.props` may not be wired up\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\nWhen a new 2026-03-11T05:22:11.2914058Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T05:21:53.341273117Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** Feature request to add an automated version bump pipeline for the Azure Management Generator (`AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` in `eng/centralpackagemanagement/Directory.Generation.Packages.props`), mirroring the existing automation for `AzureGeneratorVersion` and `UnbrandedGeneratorVersion`.\n\nFiled by a **repo member** (@ArcturusZhang). Not customer-reported.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Issue Analysis\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n**Type:** Feature request / engineering system improvement  \n**Area:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` — Azure Management Generator CI pipeline  \n**Priority indicators:** Medium — currently requires manual intervention each time a new mgmt generator build is published, creating toil and potential for version drift\n\n**Root cause / gap identified:**  \n`AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` was recently introduced but the `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` template's Regenerate stage does not yet wire up the automated NuGet package version bump to `Directory.Generation.Packages.props` for the mgmt generator. The other two generators (`UnbrandedGenerator`, `AzureGenerator`) already have this working.\n\n**Key files involved:**\n| File | Role |\n|---|---|\n| `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml` | Mgmt generator CI — already uses `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` |\n| `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/ci.yml` | Azure generator CI — reference working implementation |\n| `eng/common/pipelines/templates/archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` | Shared template with the Regenerate stage |\n| `eng/centralpackagemanagement/Directory.Generation.Packages.props` | Where `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` lives |\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔧 Suggested Tasks / Checklist\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Compare `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/ci.yml` vs `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml` to identify what parameters are passed to `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` for the Regenerate stage\n- [ ] Check if `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` already accepts a parameter to control which property in `Directory.Generation.Packages.props` gets bumped (e.g., `generatorVersionPropertyName`)\n- [ ] If the template already supports it, add/fix the parameter in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml` pointing to `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion`\n- [ ] If the template does NOT support a configurable property name yet, extend the template to accept a NuGet package version property name parameter\n- [ ] Verify the PR title format `\"Update {emitterName} version to prerelease {emitterVersion}\"` is produced correctly for the mgmt generator case\n- [ ] Test end-to-end by triggering a prerelease build of the mgmt generator and confirming the auto-bump PR is created\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e💡 Investigation Starting Points\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n1. **Diff the two CI files** to see what's different:\n   ```\n   diff eng/packages/http-client-csharp/ci.yml eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml\n   ```\n\n2. **Check the shared template** for any parameter that controls the `props` property name:\n   ```\n   grep -n \"GeneratorVersion\\|generatorVersion\\|packageVersionPropertyName\" eng/common/pipelines/templates/archetype-typespec-emitter.yml\n   ```\n\n3. **Reference PR** that introduced `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion`: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56627#discussion_r2915155700\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e👥
